title: Red Sox’ Willson Contreras apologizes for recent on-field confrontations: ‘It’s been a really tough week’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Willson Contreras apologizes for recent on-field brawls and appeals a seven-game suspension from MLB.
Two earthquakes in Venezuela, Contreras's home country, have added to his emotional stress during these incidents.
Cade Cavalli of the Nationals also apologized, acknowledging competitive tensions with Contreras on the field.

title: Red Sox Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez selected to MLB All-Star Game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox pitchers Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez selected for the 2026 MLB All-Star Game.
Chapman set a record for most career strikeouts among relievers, with a 2.10 ERA and 17 saves in 2026.
Suarez boasts a 2.94 ERA with 92 strikeouts over 88.2 innings, leading the team’s young pitching staff.

title: Paul Pierce encourages Jaylen Brown after trade from Celtics: ‘This is what keeps you hungry’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jaylen Brown was traded from the Boston Celtics to the Philadelphia 76ers, sparking commentary from Paul Pierce.
Paul Pierce encouraged Brown to use feelings of disrespect, similar to his own trade experience, as motivation.
Pierce, while disappointed by the trade, remains optimistic about Boston's future with Paul George on the roster.

title: Photos: Boston celebrates the Fourth of July
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Boston celebrated the 250th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence with a parade and ceremonial reading.
The Independence Day festivities included a large parade and gathering at the Old State House and Esplanade.
The Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular featured a drone show and fireworks display at Hatch Memorial Shell.

title: Is Caleb Durbin’s turnaround enough to solidify his future with the Red Sox?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Caleb Durbin leads Red Sox with a .977 OPS and seven home runs in the past month.
Durbin's improved performance began after working with a new hitting coach in mid-season.
Durbin's contract gives Red Sox control until 2031, aiding salary cap management for future moves.

title: Red Sox’ Aroldis Chapman breaks MLB relief pitcher strikeout record in save vs. Angels
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Aroldis Chapman surpassed Hoyt Wilhelm with 1,364 career strikeouts as a relief pitcher, breaking the MLB record.
Chapman's record strikeout occurred during Boston's 5-2 win over the Los Angeles Angels, securing the team's victory.
At 847 innings, Chapman's record came in far fewer innings than Wilhelm, who pitched 1,872.1 innings.

title: Allstate names the most collision-prone cities in the U.S.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston ranked the most collision-prone U.S. city for the second consecutive year in Allstate's 2026 report.
Worcester and Springfield ranked 197th and 196th respectively in Allstate's list of riskiest driving cities.
Brownsville, Texas, deemed the safest city, with drivers averaging nearly 15 years between collisions.

title: Falmouth man, 74, arrested after police say he led troopers on wrong-way, high-speed chase over Bourne Bridge
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Armando Deleon, 74, led police on a 105 mph chase over the Bourne Bridge before barricading himself in his home.
Deleon faced over a dozen charges including obstructing an emergency vehicle, resisting arrest, and assault with a dangerous weapon.
A judge ordered Deleon held without bail; next court appearance is scheduled for August 5 in Falmouth District Court.

title: Boston police release new photos of alleged lemonade stand robber
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston police released surveillance photos of a second juvenile linked to an armed lemonade stand robbery in South Boston.
The robbery occurred on June 10, involving the display of a firearm and theft of cash from siblings' lemonade stand.
A 14-year-old was previously arrested for armed robbery; police are seeking public help to identify another suspect.

title: Jayson Tatum and Brad Stevens appear on Rich Paul’s big board of LeBron James landing spots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: LeBron James is considering his final NBA team this offseason after informing the Lakers of his departure intentions.
Rich Paul listed potential landing spots for James, mentioning Celtics' Jayson Tatum and Brad Stevens as possibilities.
Boston acquired Mitchell Robinson and Mike Conley Jr., indicating their aggressive roster retooling this offseason.
